author.name: Aaron Wood
author.headline: Founding Product Designer of Successful Fin-tech Platform | Player/Coach | Design Systems | Usability Testing | AI Tool Integration | B2B & B2C | Regulated Industries Fin-tech & Health-tech

posts.content:
Today I experimented with the Figma + Claude integration and the speed to prototype was so much fun! I ❤️ design systems, so I presented two simple input variants (basic and currency) with three states each: Default, Focus, and Filled. I asked Claude to show me options for making the contrast between default and focus more striking, while ensuring the label is still highly readable.

Did it do what I wanted, mistake-free and passing accessibility standards, with one long detailed prompt? Definitely not. 

Did I get there in 20 minutes? Yes, and while these are React components by default, another simple prompt gets me to Vue or whatever is needed for a given project. 

What I love about this process is how quickly you can assess options relative to your existing product. I tend to be decisive and get an idea into usability testing quickly, so this workflow excites me. 

What could be a challenge for some is options fatigue. If an AI tool can show you 10 options to do a simple thing in a short amount of time, would that slow you down? Having strong taste as a designer still matters, and perhaps even more now. Curious how that plays out. 

All in all this is another solid tool to add to the tool belt, and I will enjoy using it where applicable in the future!

ai.title: Figma + Claude integration experiment: prototyping input variants with accessibility focus in 20 minutes
ai.summary: Designer experiments with Figma + Claude integration to rapidly prototype input component variants (basic and currency) with three states each. While not mistake-free initially, achieved working prototypes in 20 minutes with accessibility considerations.
ai.why_important: Shows real-world experience with new AI design tools and realistic expectations about iteration needed.
ai.learnings: Figma + Claude integration enables rapid prototyping but requires iteration for accessibility compliance and error-free output.

author.name: Carmen Rincon
author.headline: Helping others build AI-powered design workflows and upskilling designers through real startup projects at Yummy Labs

posts.content:
Hey everyone! If you're curious about Claude Design workflows, I'm sharing my personal workflow (part 1 & 2) now. Everything I've done, step by step 👀

I've used it for building my interactive portfolio site, creating AI-ready design systems from scratch, and complex product work for clients. It's works whether I'm designing a single component or an entire app.

➡️Part 1: The Setup How to connect Claude to Figma, structure your design tokens so AI can actually read them, build Claude Skills, and set up a Project with real context. This is the foundation. Skip it and nothing else works properly.

➡️Part 2: The Design Workflow How to give Claude full product context (not just tokens), choose the right model, plan a build before designing, iterate inside the chat to save tokens, and push to Figma with your variables connected.

The timing is kind of perfect because Figma just announced (literally this week) that AI agents can now write directly to your Figma canvas. Not just read your file. Actually create components, apply variables, build with your design system. Through the official MCP. Both guides cover this update.

🥶A few things I learned the hard way:

>Don't jump into Figma right away. Claude uses a lot of tokens when writing via MCP. Iterate inside the chat first using artifacts or inline visuals, get the design right, then push to Figma when you're happy.

>Always ask Claude to plan the build before it starts designing. Which components exist? Which ones need to be created? What states are needed? Adjusting a plan is way cheaper than redoing a finished component.

>And when Claude creates things in Figma, ask it explicitly to connect everything to your existing variables. Otherwise it hardcodes values and you're reconnecting tokens yourself.

I also update these guides regularly as I learn better approaches and as tools get updated (like the Figma write access this week). So they'll keep getting better over time.

Part 3 drops next week: from Claude Chat to Claude Code.

📎 Workflow Part 1: https://lnkd.in/e5uR6wGt
📎 Workflow Part 2:https://lnkd.in/e5ddkcJ8

Follow for more design AI tips 🤖

ai.title: Claude Design workflows step-by-step: portfolio site, design systems, and complex product work
ai.workflow_stage: concept / ideation | asset creation | layout / composition
ai.summary: Comprehensive guide sharing personal Claude Design workflow used for interactive portfolio sites, AI-ready design systems from scratch, and complex client product work. Covers setup, Figma connection, design token structure, Claude Skills, and project context.
ai.why_important: Provides detailed, practical workflow that can be replicated for real projects across different scales.
ai.learnings: How to structure design tokens for AI readability, set up Claude Skills, and build effective AI design project context.

author.name: Yossi Refaeli
author.headline: Engineering Mindset & Visual ai Design @ Reality Labs

posts.content:
I’ve been experimenting with a local pipeline that feels a bit like cheating.

Inspired by the workflow shared by Mickmumpitz, combine raw Blender renders and cinematography using ComfyUI and the SkyReels V3 + Wan VACE model merge.

The traditional 3D pipeline is great, but sometimes you just want to play Digital Puppet Theater and let the AI be your VFX department.

The Workflow 
The Skeleton (Blender) I block out the scene, camera moves, and basic geometry. No textures, no fancy lighting just the raw clay. 

I run a Look Dev pass on a single frame to dial in the vibe before committing to a full render.

The AI takes my 3D blueprint and fills in the rest generating realistic simulation and textures that stay pinned to my geometry.

Why this is a game changer
Zero UV Unwrapping, Dynamic FX for Free, Instant Style Swaps

ai.title: Local pipeline: Blender renders + ComfyUI cinematography with SkyReels V3 + Wan VACE model merge
ai.summary: Experimental workflow combining raw Blender renders with AI cinematography using ComfyUI and specific model merges. Blocks out scenes and camera moves in Blender, then uses AI as VFX department for enhancement.
ai.why_important: Shows hybrid approach where 3D software handles structure while AI enhances visual quality and cinematography.
ai.learnings: How to combine traditional 3D blocking with AI enhancement for cinematographic results without full 3D finishing.

author.name: Satrayana Eldin de Pécoulas
author.headline: 3D Generalist & Creative Technologist | Exploring AI Workflow Prototyping | Senior Content Creator @ Wonderlabs

posts.content:
🚀 Introducing ComfyUI Yedp Action Director V9.20 

When I first developed the **Yedp Action Director** node for ComfyUI, the goal was simple: solve the temporal consistency problem in AI Video generation.

I wanted a way to take standard **Mixamo MoCap animations**, drop them into an AI workflow, and instantly bake perfectly synchronized ControlNet passes (OpenPose, Depth). By driving Stable Diffusion with raw 3D data instead of video-to-video estimations, we completely eliminate the flickering and morphing that plagues GenAI.

Today, I’m thrilled to announce a massive update that evolves the tool from a character utility into a comprehensive 3D scene compositor, fully bridging the gap between professional 3D DCCs (Maya/Blender), Mixamo, and AI generation.

Here is what you can now do entirely inside ComfyUI:

🕺 **Mixamo Auto-Retargeting:** Create up to 16 characters with FBX/GLB animations. The engine automatically maps Mixamo rigs to the internal skeleton.

💡 **Custom Lighting Engine:** Light your scene with configurable Ambient, Directional, Point, and Spot lights that cast real-time shadows.

🎬 **6-in-1 ControlNet Baking:** Automatically render pixel-perfect batches of OpenPose, Depth, Normal, Canny, Shaded, and Alpha Matte passes in a single click.

🌍 **Environment Integration:** Seamlessly import `.fbx` and `.glb` environments with dynamic object animations.

🌪️ **Real-Time Baked Physics:** Support for Shape Keys/Morph Targets allows you to bring baked cloth, wind, and soft-body simulations straight into the browser.

🎥 **Professional Camera Pipeline:** Import animated camera tracks natively. Includes a Camera Override system, a ghost-proxy visualizer, and a coordinate fixer to instantly translate Maya's scales and Z-up axes to WebGL.

If you're a Tech Artist, Animator, or AI Filmmaker looking for absolute, mathematical control over your GenAI pipelines, I'd love for you to check out the repository. Let me know how you integrate it into your workflows! 👇

https://lnkd.in/eKjJSrd4

ai.title: ComfyUI Yedp Action Director V9.20: Mixamo MoCap to AI Video with temporal consistency
ai.summary: Major update to ComfyUI node that solves temporal consistency in AI video by converting Mixamo MoCap animations into synchronized ControlNet passes (OpenPose, Depth), eliminating flickering by using 3D data instead of video-to-video estimation.
ai.why_important: Addresses core technical challenge in AI video production with innovative approach using 3D motion capture data.
ai.learnings: How to achieve temporal consistency in AI video generation by driving Stable Diffusion with raw 3D motion data rather than video estimation.

author.name: Cheryl Harris
author.headline: Design Leader | AI-Enabled Creative Workflows | Global Brand & Campaign Systems | AI Film Production

posts.content:
Another AI video test! 

I've called this short cinematic test, “Naomi (vs the Machine,)'  The main goal was to experiment with dynamic camera movement (which has been a hard crash course in cinema).  I experimented  with low angles, tracking shots, and fast action cuts. Treating AI video more like directing a scene than generating a single image.

One of the biggest challenges was maintaining character consistency across multiple shots, particularly with afro textured hair and darker skin tones under changing lighting and fast paced motion. This is something I have originally found lacking in some tools, but some of the AI models I played with were surprisingly half decent.  It did however required a lot of iteration to keep the characters feeling coherent from clip to clip. There are definite differences, but I found to keep the action moving quickly, I had to sacrifice a degree of consistency.

Another takeaway was how important editing and post production still are. Even with AI generated footage, the final result relied heavily on manual work in Premiere to stitch the action together and reframe shots. 

Overall a really fun project to work on. Will add some of the key learnings and the workflow to flashafro.com soon.

#GenerativeAI #CreativeTechnology #AIStorytelling
#CreativeExperimentation

ai.title: Naomi (vs the Machine): experimenting with dynamic camera movement and character consistency in AI video
ai.summary: Cinematic AI video test focusing on dynamic camera movement with low angles, tracking shots, and fast action cuts. Treating AI video generation more like directing scenes than generating single images, with specific attention to character consistency challenges.
ai.why_important: Addresses representation and technical challenges in AI video generation, particularly for darker skin tones and textured hair.
ai.learnings: How to approach AI video generation as scene direction and tackle character consistency issues across multiple shots with diverse representation.

posts.content:
AI video used to struggle with structure. Here the new workaround people are using: use a basic 3D animation to guide the structural details and motion of AI video generation. I used a viewport render of a medicine capsule animation I made a while ago in C4D as an example.

Tools I used: Kling O1 Reference Video to Video, NanoBanana 2, Nano Banana Pro, Weavy, and C4D.

PROCESS:
1. Export a viewport render of animation from 3D software.
2. Extract first frame from viewport render.
3. Input first frame alongside a visual style reference into image model to generate stylised first frame (look dev).
4. Prompt Kling Video Reference to apply 3D viewport animation to the stylised first frame to generate your AI video.
5. Do any final adjustments in software like AfterEffects (if needed).

THOUGHTS:
- This method solves a big missing piece in AI video outputs (structural control).
- Good example of how using a hybrid of AI and traditional techniques gives you the most out of AI generation.
- Huge potential future use in media like 3D motion design, VFX, games, architecture visualisations etc.

Also, comment “Creative Futures Lab” to get free access to the last few spots of my creative AI course (April cohort).

#weavy #nanobananapro #kling #3Danimation #motiondesign #creativeAI #AI

ai.title: AI video structural workaround: using 3D viewport renders to guide motion and details in video generation
ai.summary: Workflow using basic 3D viewport renders to guide structural details and motion in AI video generation. Process involves exporting viewport render, extracting first frame, generating stylized version, then using reference video for AI video generation.
ai.why_important: Solves AI video's structural weakness by using 3D animation as foundation for more controlled results.
ai.learnings: How to use 3D software viewport renders as reference structure for more controlled AI video generation with better motion and detail consistency.

author.name: soheil sigurdsson
author.headline: Creative Director | AI-Enhanced Visual Production | Lead 3D Artist | Media & Graphic Design Specialist

posts.content:
I spent a month investigating about different workflows using CGAI (CGI + AI) to make automotive animations look good on screen. 

Not CGI. Not full AI. Something in between.

This short video is the result — three vehicles, three environments, one story:

→ A red Ford Mustang GT cutting through a wet city at night
→ A Ford Ranger alone in the desert, dust and heat
→ A classic 70´s Ford Mustang — desert road, different era

The workflow is hybrid: 3D animation for precision and control (Unreal Engine and Blender), AI tools for photorealistic enhancement. Every vehicle was built and animated in 3D first. The environments and atmosphere came after.

This is my first piece built entirely on that pipeline. I plan to dive deeper and keep improving with new tools and model updates.

I'd love to hear what you think. 

#AutomotiveFilm #3DAnimation #AIAnimation #MotionDesign #CinematicReel

ai.title: CGAI automotive workflow: 3D animation precision + AI photorealistic enhancement for three Ford vehicles
ai.summary: Month-long investigation into CGAI (CGI + AI) workflows for automotive animations. Hybrid approach using 3D animation for precision and control, AI tools for photorealistic enhancement. Features three Ford vehicles in different environments with complete storytelling.
ai.why_important: Shows professional hybrid approach combining 3D precision with AI enhancement for automotive visualization.
ai.learnings: How to balance 3D animation control with AI enhancement for photorealistic automotive content while maintaining narrative structure.
